_meta.notes: Updated via Claude Code conversation. Edit by talking to Claude or directly editing this file.

exclusions.reason: Employment equity + blackout periods. Already heavily concentrated via RSUs. Hard exclusion — agent must never act on Tesla in Robinhood.

space.thesis: Artemis program returning humans to the moon + SpaceX driving down orbital launch costs = orbital infrastructure buildout. Satellite constellations, lunar economy, US government contracts will grow substantially. Payoff is long but expected to be large.
space.quality_filter: Company must directly benefit from cheap orbital access or government space contracts. Sub-orbital tourism (different, unproven market) is excluded from this thesis.

notes.RKLB: Highest current conviction. Operational launch vehicle (Electron), Neutron in development, US govt contracts. Directly aligned with thesis. Continue accumulating on dips.
notes.ASTS: AST SpaceMobile — direct-to-device satellite broadband. Higher risk than RKLB but legitimate thesis. AT&T/Vodafone partnerships. Appropriate as smaller position.
notes.SPCE: DO NOT ADD. Sub-orbital tourist flights from New Mexico — completely misaligned with the orbital infrastructure thesis. Went through restructuring. Pending sell decision (Mon 2026-06-30).
notes.ARKX: Space ETF. Diversified exposure. Long hold.

ev_transition.thesis: ICE vehicles become a minority within 20-30 years. Near-certainty over 50-year horizon. Growth will be gradual not exponential, but companies that survive the shakeout and achieve scale will be very large.
ev_transition.quality_filter: Prioritize companies with path to profitability and real manufacturing scale. Chinese ADR geopolitical risk (potential delisting, audit disputes) must be factored into position sizing.

notes.BYDDY: Cleaner expression of China EV thesis than NIO. Profitable, vertically integrated (batteries + vehicles), dominant market share. Prefer adding here over NIO.
notes.NIO: HOLD ONLY. Do not add without a documented fresh catalyst: profitability milestone, major new partnership, or volume growth clearly outpacing BYD. Risks: cash burn, BYD price competition in premium segment, China ADR geopolitical exposure. 54 buys already — averaging-down trap acknowledged.
notes.LCID: HOLD ONLY. Small position ($1.5K). At cost basis. Not adding.
notes.CHPT: HOLD ONLY. Last buy at $0.58 — effectively written off. Not adding.

cannabis.thesis: US federal legalization was expected to unlock market economics. Thesis has stalled: Section 280E tax burden cripples legal operators, no federal banking access, legal market undercut by untaxed black market, Gen Z consumption lower than expected.

notes.CRON: HOLD ONLY. Cronos Group — Canadian-listed, Altria-backed, one of the more resilient cannabis companies. Small position (~$1.6K). Not adding. Revisit only if US federal legislation materially advances (banking access + Section 280E reform).

ai_infrastructure.thesis: AI compute demand is secular and accelerating. Data centers, GPUs, networking infrastructure will see sustained capital expenditure regardless of which AI models win.
ai_infrastructure.quality_filter: Prefer picks-and-shovels plays over pure AI application companies. Look for companies with durable competitive moats in the supply chain.

notes.NVDA: Core holding. Dominant GPU market share for AI training/inference. Continue accumulating on meaningful dips (>15% off recent high, RSI < 40).
notes.AMD: Secondary GPU/CPU play. Benefits from NVDA supply constraints and data center diversification.
notes.GOOGL: Largest open position. TPU infrastructure, cloud AI services, search moat. Long-term hold.
notes.AMZN: AWS + Trainium/Inferentia custom chips. Consistent accumulation.

DAL.thesis: Travel demand recovery post-COVID. Airlines operationally improved. Long-term hold at current cost basis.
DAL.action: No new buys. Reassess if airline fundamentals deteriorate.

KO.thesis: Dividend compounder. Steady accumulation via Robinhood recurring buys. Defensive position.
KO.action: Continue small recurring buys. Not a high-conviction growth play.

UBER.thesis: Rideshare + delivery network effects. Path to profitability achieved. Autonomous vehicle wildcard.
UBER.action: Hold. Revisit if autonomous vehicle timeline accelerates (positive) or Waymo expands rapidly (competitive risk).

NFLX.thesis: Streaming consolidation winner. Ad-supported tier expanding TAM. Password sharing crackdown worked.

CRM.thesis: Enterprise software. AI integration (Einstein) could re-accelerate growth.
CRM.action: Hold. Small position relative to cost.

BA.thesis: Aerospace duopoly. Supply chain issues resolving. Long recovery runway.
BA.action: Hold. Do not add until 737 MAX production ramp is clearly on track.

ARKK.thesis: Innovation ETF. Diversified exposure to disruptive tech. Bought during 2021 peak — underwater.
ARKK.action: Hold as diversified long-term bet. Not adding.

SMR.thesis: NuScale Power — small modular nuclear reactors. Data center energy demand could be a major catalyst.
SMR.action: Small speculative position. Hold. Watch for SMR regulatory approvals and data center partnerships.

BB.thesis: BlackBerry pivot to enterprise security software. Original meme-stock buy.
BB.action: Small legacy position. Not adding. Monitor security software traction.

NOK.thesis: 5G infrastructure. Originally a meme play, now holding on 5G rollout thesis.

USO.thesis: Oil ETF. Original COVID recovery play from 2020.
USO.action: Old position. Reassess relevance given EV conviction.

hard_constraints: No averaging down: max 3 additional buys on any position >35% below average cost basis without explicit user re-approval of thesis
Hold-only names (NIO, LCID, CHPT, CRON): no new buys without documented fresh catalyst
Do-not-add names (SPCE): no new buys under any circumstances
Max single-name position: 10% of total Robinhood portfolio value
Max single speculative theme: 15% of total portfolio (e.g., all space names excluding core ETFs)
profit_take_triggers: Flag for review when any position is up >60% from average cost basis
Flag for review when any single name exceeds 15% of portfolio value
entry_criteria: Position in convictions.json (theme approved or individual holding)
Quality filter passed: revenue growth >10% YoY, gross margin stable/improving, no distressed balance sheet
Technical signal: >15% below 52-week high AND RSI < 40
Dip cause: macro/sector selloff preferred over company-specific bad news
